CN: 她的缥缈的身影叫人想起振翅欲飞的鸥鸟，白裙被猎猎海风扬起的时候，观者总能描绘出一副飞向乌托邦的飞鸟之图。\n不知道那端坐于桅杆横木之上的少女，是否也曾想象过自己的遨游之姿呢？
EN: Her ethereal beauty is always reminiscent of the bird of heaven, spreading its glossy wings towards utopia as her white dress flutters with caressing breezes.\nHowever, does the girl who perches on the mast ever imagine herself flying in the azure sky?

CN: 菲茵特是一粒褪色的、难以破壳的种子。\n人们因她的残缺而怜悯她，因她的虚弱而爱怜她。\n知道她是自愿留在壳中之人，已然化作齑粉，成为星之尘埃。
EN: Faint is a faded, hard-to-hatch seed.\nPeople pity her for her flaws and love her for her weaknesses.\nKnowing this, she chose to remain in the shell, now turned to dust—stardust.

CN:
这对姐妹中，有一人是不该出生的存在。
在无法被观测的天琴座荒星上，菲茵特与汀克特在恶言中度过了半生。
但究竟谁是那个不被承认的存在呢？
姐姐菲茵特强大却无法自控，所掠取的生命力总像河流般逝去，只能靠舔舐残留的露珠勉强度日。
妹妹汀克特美丽却难于自立，如浮萍般倔强却无处扎根的生命，只得围绕着姐姐回旋。
也许，她们彼此都认为自己是不该存在的那人，只是菲茵特更早做出了决断——以自我了断做威胁，她将妹妹强行推上了离开的旅程。
「离开吧，汀克特。不要让我绊住你。」
「你要在星间歌唱，成为最受瞩目的色彩。」
EN:
Among these sisters, one of them should never have been born.
On the unobservable Lyra desolate star, Faint and Tinct spent half their lives exchanging bitter words.
But who is the unacknowledged one?
Sister Faint is powerful yet uncontrollable; the life force she seizes seems to flow away like a river, leaving her only able to barely survive by licking the remaining dewdrops.
Sister Tinct is beautiful yet struggles to be independent; her life is as stubborn as a floating weed, yet rooted nowhere, causing her to revolve around her sister.
Perhaps they both believe they are the one who should not exist. It's just that Faint made her decision earlier — threatening self-termination, she forced her sister onto the journey of departure.
"Leave, Tinct. Don't let me hold you back."
"You shall sing among the stars, becoming the most eye-catching color."

CN: 必须留心，她那冷静、知性的话语具有着令人失神的魅力。不要好奇异形的头颅之下是否有另一副容貌，只需了解她对于这副类人的身体抱有长久的不满。\n她的灯火曾诱引着飞蛾，也曾将迷途者置于死地，而如今那其中似乎正透露出迷途者的茫然。
EN: Beware: a siren’s mesmerizing charm lurks under her seemingly calm and rational words. Never show curiosity towards the unique appearance beneath her malformed helmet; never forget her long-lasting dissatisfaction with this humanoid body.\nHer gleaming light once lured moths to its molten warmth. Her burning fire once guided the lost to their doom. Now, only confusion flickers in her lighthouse.

CN: 他是名副其实的巨人。强健的肉体，昭示着他那令人畏怖的力量。他使用着一柄无人能够举起的巨刃，战争与血祭的滋养，令它的表面如同饱涂油脂的皮毛那般光亮。巨刃的獠牙曾斩下过无数敌人、同族的头颅，亦斩下过他自己的。
EN: He is but a giant. His silhouette aweing, his power indestructible. His weapon a colossal blade that no one else can lift. His victories, gleaming like oiled fur on the blade’s fang, nourished by countless wars and savage sacrifices. His prize, a mountain of heads severed from his enemies and kin, crowned only by his own head on top.

CN: 渴盼着名为「母亲」之物，服从「主人」命令的少女。\n世界遗忘了她的名字，于是那在巨型黑色淤泥眼球中，如百合花般绽放的脆弱白色纤影，被后来者仓促命名为「Lily」。
EN: Yearning for an entity she calls "Mommy", this girl obediently follows the commands of her "Master".\nHer true name lost to the world, she is now known only by the form she inhabits: a delicate white filament, reminiscent of a lily, blooming at the center of a giant black eyeball made of sludge. Hence, they named her Lily.

CN: 萝坦身上贴了太多太多标签，没人知道该用怎样的态度来与这名黑衣剑士相处。\n太过谦卑，她会以无趣之名敲碎你的膝盖；太过傲慢，她会用巨剑斩断你的无礼；太过强硬，她会用战斗软化你的灵魂。\n但也许，这正是星球初生之子的傲慢：作为曾经的守护者，她理应享有傲慢的权利。
EN: Lotan has so many labels attached to her that no one knows how to approach this black-clad swordswoman.\nToo humble and she will shatter your knees for being too boring; too arrogant and she will slash your insolence with her greatsword; too tough and she will soften your soul through battle.\nBut perhaps this is the arrogance of the planet’s first-born. As a former guardian, she deserves her pride.

CN: 高傲、任性、自我中心……许多与她接触过的人，都会对她的个性留下如此评价。她在以往生活的环境中，似乎一直受到众星捧月般的照顾，也许正是这些曾经的无微不至养成了她骄纵的性格。\n「喂！你在看什么东西？不是说过了吗，叫你三声之内就必须回应本小姐！」\n——阅读档案的时候也得十分注意。
EN: Proud, willful, self-centered... Many who have interacted with her would describe her personality as such. She seems to have always been treated like the center of the universe in her last living arrangement; perhaps it was this meticulous care that cultivated her spoiled nature.\n“Hey! What are you looking at? Didn’t I say that if I call you three times, you have to respond to this young lady?!”\n—One must be very careful, even when reading the documents.

CN: ·狂气爆发和技能都具有随机性，运气越好收益越高。\n·灵知觉醒可获得或选择升级「混沌的遗赠」。\n·在本次关卡中，将携带等级 50 级，技能等级5，启灵3 的莱克和萝坦出战，初始莱克拥有 25% 暴击率加成、所有的「混沌的遗赠」造物与额外一套卡牌，敌人第一回合获得临时强化。
EN: Ryker's Exalt and Skills yield randomized results; the better your luck, the greater the rewards.\nRouse grants or allows selection of upgrades for "Chaotic Legacy".\nIn this stage, Ryker and Lotan will be deployed at Level 50, Skill Level 5, Enlighten Level 3. Ryker initially has a 25% Crit. Rate boost, all "Chaotic Legacy" Relics, and an additional set of Cards. The enemy receives Temporary STRengthening in the first round.

CN:
莱克被他的警探好友称为「好运侦探」。
顾名思义，他是个好运的人：只要下注，就能赢回十倍本金；只要靠蒙，就能选对答案。
好运侦探不是非得当侦探，只是误打误撞侦破了多起疑案，就被人簇拥着走上了这条道路。
他破案不靠推理，靠运气。只要在案发现场巡视一周，就能撞上犯人；只要走在路上，就会捡到关键证据。
在这样的好运中，他度过了顺风顺水的二十五年。
从来不为昨日烦恼，从来不为明日发愁。
EN:
Ryker is known as the "Lucky Detective" by his detective friends.
As the name suggests, he is a lucky person: whenever he places a bet, he wins ten times the stake; whenever he guesses, he chooses the right answer.
The "Lucky Detective" doesn’t have to be an actual detective; he‘s solved many cases by chance alone, so he was embraced on this path.
He doesn’t solve cases by deduction, relying on luck instead. Just a tour around the crime scene and he stumbles upon the culprit; just walking along the road, he picks up key evidence.
With such good fortune, he's lived out a smooth twenty-five years.
He never worries about yesterday, nor frets about tomorrow.

CN: 来自西岱的艺术家并不在意人类，她只追求某种绝对意义上的纯粹之美，将转瞬即逝的刹那铭刻为永恒。\n灵感之泉奔涌而出，刻刀从未停歇。每完成一个作品，珊瑚色的阴霾便蚕食躯体一分，痛苦与喜悦并生，而雕砌者亦不在意自己。
EN: The artist from Cité cares not for other people. She only pursues the absolute form of pure beauty, etching ephemeral moments into eternity. \n As her inspiration surges forth, her chisel never ceases; as her works witness completion, her body is submerged in coral-colored shades. In the corner of her workshop which she pays no heed, her agony and delight intertwine, sprouting from her very existence.

CN: 如果你的记忆没有被人动过手脚，三十分钟前你应该还在自己小巧精致的书桌前，处理光怪陆离的报告。
EN: If your memory hasn't been tampered with, you should still be at your delicate little desk, dealing with bizarre reports as you were thirty minutes ago.

CN: 虽然这里完全比不上你房间中松软的大床，但温暖的床铺还是可以让你舒服地瘫在上面。
EN: Although it can't compare to the big, soft bed in your room, the warm bedding still allows you to comfortably sprawl out.

CN: 提灯……教会？我们今天的目标就是他们么？
EN: Church of the Lightbearers...? Are they our target?

CN: 没，只是感觉有些阴森森的……而且大姐，这次行动会不会太顺利了。
EN: No, it just feels a bit eerie... and Kath, isn't this operation going too smoothly?

CN: 虽然我已经失言了……
EN: I misspoke there...

CN: 现场气氛突然变得有些凝重，失去同伴的悲伤感在你心底弥漫。
EN: The atmosphere in the tent suddenly became tense; the sorrow of losing companions spread in your heart.

CN: 这其中，自然包括杀死您手下这种，在他们眼里一文不值的小老鼠。
EN: This naturally includes killing the likes of your subordinates; those they view as worthless little mice.

CN: 尽管已经从记忆中得知，但当你真的看到这个场面时还是感到十分诧异。
EN: Although you'd learned this from your memories, seeing the scene firsthand still left you quite astonished.

CN: 一点钟方向，准备突围，伊斯梅尔！
EN: One o'clock, prepare to break out, Ishmael!

CN: 蒂卡和路易斯迅速站到队伍两侧，挥舞利刃将敌人成片斩断。
EN: Tica and Louis quickly took positions on either side of the line, wielding their blades to sever enemies to pieces.

CN: 不知是否是把护卫都派出去的缘故，浩大的神殿内抵抗相当微弱。
EN: You don't know if it's because all the guards were sent out, but the resistance in the grand temple is quite weak.

CN: 老者伸出枯皱的手拼命向丢失的权杖抓去，嘶哑和扭曲的声音顺着畸形的声带蠕动爬出。
EN: The old man reached out his withered hand, desperately trying to grasp the lost scepter, a hoarse, distorted voice crawling out along his deformed vocal cords.

CN: 凯蒂古拉重重一脚踩下，将对方的话按回喉咙里。
EN: Kathigu-Ra stomped down hard, forcing her opponent's words back into his throat.

CN: 凯蒂古拉将弹药填充进枪膛，击锤被缓缓压下。
EN: Kathigu-Ra loaded the ammo into the chamber and slowly pressed down the hammer.

CN: 无视正在发生生理痉挛的尸体，凯蒂古拉在对方绸缎织成的衣物上擦了擦溅到枪上的组织液。
EN: Ignoring the body in physiological spasm, Kathigu-Ra wiped the tissue fluid splattered on the gun on the man's satin clothing.

CN: 地面开始剧烈的震动，你能感觉到不止是神殿内部，整个提灯教会所在地都陷入了某种异变之中。
EN: The ground began to tremble violently; you could feel that not only the temple's interior but the entire Temple Light Church was undergoing some kind of anomaly.

CN: 很好，带着我们原路返回，尽量减少看到活的敌人。
EN: Great, let's return the way we came and try to minimize encounters with live enemies.

CN: 手持镰刀或无头的怪物替代了尸体的位置，在整个空间中咆哮着。
EN: Headless monsters with scythes showed up where the corpses had been, roaring throughout the space.

CN: 娇小的少年闻言一愣，思索再三后，沉默地点了点头。
EN: The young man was momentarily stunned, but after a moment's contemplation, he nodded silently.

CN: 众人依依不舍地扭过头，收起担忧的目光，迅速沿着小径离开这里。
EN: Everyone reluctantly turned their heads away, ending their worried gazes, and quickly retreated along the path.

CN: ……第27次诱导，敌人减少至1/138
EN: ...27th engagement, enemies reduced to 1/138

CN: 就不能是单纯想看看，人类在这种情况下的表情么？
EN: Couldn't it be that I just wanted to see what kind of expression humans have in such a situation?

CN: 看来凯蒂古拉女士管教无方啊。
EN: It seems Lady Kathigu-Ra doesn't know how to manage her people.

CN: 呼……比预想中还要强。
EN: Huh... He was stronger than expected.

CN: 蒂卡不知找了什么话题，跟路易斯聊了起来。
EN: Tica found some topic to chat with Louis about.

CN: 伊斯梅尔还是跟往常一样沉默寡言，但是你注意到他悄悄地抓住了凯蒂古拉大衣的一角。
EN: Ishmael remained as quiet and reserved as ever, but you noticed him quietly grabbing a corner of Kathigu-Ra's coat.

CN: 哎，结果还是这样么。
EN: So, this is how it ends after all.

CN: 但你应该知道，我最不能容忍的是，你妄图让刀尖沾染我最重要之人的鲜血。
EN: But you should know, the one thing I cannot tolerate is your attempt to stain your blade's tip with the blood of one I cherish the most.

CN: 继续赶路吧，起码要先找到一个休息的地方。
EN: Let's keep moving, we need to at least find a place to rest first.

CN: 就你还想让我们背叛大姐么？
EN: You wanna make us betray our Big Sis? Not in your lifetime.

CN: 正当凯蒂古拉为留在这里照顾蒂卡找理由时，眼前突然变化的一切让她愣在原地。
EN: Just as Kathigu-Ra was searching for reasons to stay and take care of Tica, the sudden change of everything before her eyes left her frozen in place.

CN: 当理智终于战胜欲望，重新回到那疲惫不堪的大脑时，你早已吃下过量的食物。
EN: When reason finally triumphed over desire and returned to your exhausted mind, you had already consumed an excessive amount of food.

CN: 卢修斯将酒桶放在几人中央，淡淡酒香刺激着你们的神经。
EN: Lucius placed the wine barrels in the center of the group, and the faint aroma of wine stimulated your nerves.

CN: 不知是否是之前三十……或许是四十小时滴水未进的经历，你觉得这酒的味道远超记忆中所有美酒。
EN: You weren't sure if it was due to the previous thirty... or perhaps forty hours without a drop of water, but you found the taste of this liquor far surpassed all fine wines you could remember.

CN: 伊斯梅尔喝得并不快，相比于其他人地豪饮，伊斯梅尔更多的是让其缓缓从喉咙流下。
EN: Ishmael didn't drink quickly; compared to the others' heavy drinking, he preferred to let it slowly flow down his throat.

CN: 说起来，我们好像都不知道其他人是怎么碰到大姐的。
EN: Speaking of which, it seems none of us know how the others met Big Sis.

CN: 我也没见过，每次都是大姐回来时身边多了个人，然后告诉我们从今往后大家就要一起生活了。
EN: I've never seen it either; every time, Big Sis returned with someone new, then told us that we would all live together from now on.

CN: 这都什么时候的陈年旧事。
EN: That was all so long ago.

CN: 你们怎么还记得，我早都忘干净了。
EN: How do you all still remember? I've forgotten it all.

CN: 我的出身比大家好一点，算是有父母吧。
EN: My background is a bit better than everyone else's; I had parents at least.

CN: 但我的父母是两个混账，整天不是赌博就是吸毒，没事干就死命地打我。
EN: But my parents were two bastards who gambled and did drugs all day long, and when they had nothing else to do, they beat me mercilessly.

CN: 那是一个雷雨的早晨，我被雷声惊醒，正打算看看碗里有没有他们剩下的食物。
EN: It was a stormy morning. I was awakened by the thunder and was about to check if there was any leftover food in the bowl.

CN: 大姐就是小时候才这样吧，现在反而不会了。
EN: Big Sis was like that when we were kids, but now she doesn't act that way anymore.

CN: 这听起来就是什么传说故事中苦命主角的称号，当时我震撼得不得了！
EN: That sounds like the title of a tragic character from some legendary story; I was absolutely shocked at the time!

CN: 你当时沉死了……我本来以为会很轻的，走到一半就后悔了。
EN: You were heavy as lead back then... I thought you would be light, but halfway through, I regretted it.

CN: 是啊，总不能让你自己爬回去吧，所以只能硬着头皮把你背回去了。
EN: Yes, I couldn't let you crawl back alone, so I had to grit my teeth and carry you back.

CN: 结果没多久就被一些流里流气的人堵住了，我就拼命地跑啊跑啊。
EN: As a result I was soon blocked by some shady people, so I ran for my life desperately.

CN: 这时候我才知道，原来救了我的是一个没比我大几岁的姐姐。
EN: At that point, I discovered that the one who saved me was a girl only a few years older than me.

CN: 大姐你的气势太酷了！虽然你当时拿的只是便宜货，但毫——不影响你的帅气身姿！
EN: Kath, you were so cool! That cheap gun you had back then didnn't take away from how awesome you were.

CN: 嘿嘿，大姐不就是么，我们的心可都被你拐走了哦。
EN: Hehe, aren't you our Big Sis after all? You've already stolen all our hearts~

CN: 我就没有卢修斯那么精彩啦，我跟凯蒂古拉姐姐的故事是发生在一个小巷子里。
EN: I'm not as exciting as Lucius. My story with Big Sis Kath happened in a small alley.

CN: 那时我偷窃被抓到，在巷子里被失主打的头破血流。
EN: At that time, I was caught stealing and was beaten bloody in an alley by the owner.

CN: 当时我就在想，神啊，求求你救救我吧。
EN: At that time, I thought, God, please save me.

CN: 然后奇迹就出现了！凯蒂古拉姐姐像天使一样出现在我的眼前！
EN: And then a miracle happened! Big Sis Kath appeared before me like an angel!

CN: 怎么会呢！当时凯蒂古拉姐姐你抓住对方的手，用补偿金打发走对方，在我看来就是神明派来的天使啊。
EN: How could that be! At that time, Big Sis Kath, you grabbed the owner's hand and sent them away with compensation. To me, you felt like an angel sent by the gods.

CN: 然后凯蒂古拉姐姐就带着我，去把我的上级杀掉了。
EN: Then Big Sis Kath took me to kill my superior.

CN: 遗憾的是最后只有我一个人跟着凯蒂古拉姐姐走了，剩下的还是回去做了老本行。
EN: Unfortunately, in the end only I left with Big Sis Kath, while the others returned to their former ways.

CN: 是啊，当时我也担心会不会给大家带来麻烦，多亏凯蒂古拉姐姐~
EN: Yeah, at that time I was also worried about causing trouble for everyone. Thank God for Big Sis Kath~

CN: 他靠着我跟他打配合，在赌局中赢了不少钱。
EN: He collaborated with me, winning quite a bit of money gambling.

CN: 其实那个时候我也出千了，只是没人发现而已。毕竟赌徒只有在赌桌上，才会有钱还那些烂账。
EN: In fact, I was cheating at that time too, just no one noticed. After all, gamblers will only have the money to repay their debts at the gambling table.

CN: 每只怪物头上都有一个弹孔，精准的一击毙命。
EN: Every monster lay still, each bearing a single, precise bullet hole in its head—a perfect, merciful kill shot.

CN: 或许可以不带之一？算了，这都不关键！
EN: It might even be the best? Never mind, that's not the point!

CN: 真是聒噪，都这个时候了，还在喋喋不休个不停。
EN: How noisy. Even now, you're still chattering non-stop.

CN: 那是造物主刻在一切存在最深处、最原始也是最后的必将降临的最终恐惧——<Big:<Red:死亡>>。
EN: That was the ultimate fear, The Arrival of the deepest, most primal and final <Big:<Red:Death>> etched by the Creator in the core of all existence.

CN: 还是哀嚎更适合你，不是么？
EN: You're much more pleasant to listen to when you wail like this.

CN: 硬鞋底落在墓园的石砖路面，清晰的脚步声越过密密麻麻的坟墓，传到雾色的尽头。
EN: Hard soles hit the stone path of the cemetery. The clear sound of footsteps passes through densely packed graves, carried along the fog.

CN: 脚步声停下。你抬起头，一栋崎岖的别墅矗立在墓园之中，如同一座造型奇特的巨大墓碑。
EN: The footsteps stop. You lift your head; a rugged villa looms in the cemetery, resembling a massive, uniquely shaped tombstone.

CN: 各色的暗淡颜料被涂抹在漆黑、古老的墙面。一场废弃舞池中的漫长狂欢。
EN: Assorted dull pigments are smeared across its ancient, pitch-black walls—a lasting revelry on an abandoned dance floor.

CN: 这场梦境的开场倒是别开生面。
EN: The opening of this dream is quite refreshing.

CN: 埃弗，美术学院绘画系大一的学生，革新派画法的拥护者……
EN: Avery, a freshman Painting major at the Art Academy and supporter of innovative painting techniques…

CN: 你闭上眼睛，仔细回忆着自己的身份。一个熟悉的名字窜入你的脑海。
EN: You close your eyes and carefully recall your identity. A familiar name flashes in your mind.

CN: 大门被敲响。你望向别墅的左侧，那里没有门牌，只有一个粗犷的墓碑，上面用血污般的颜料涂抹出一个丑陋的名字。
EN: There's a knock at the gate. You look to the left side of the villa, but there's no doorplate to be found—only a rough tombstone, upon which a name is grotesquely rendered in paint like dried blood.

CN: 与你同班的年轻画家兴奋地打开门，乱糟糟的头发与衣服上沾染着各色的颜料。这栋别墅毫无争议的主人。
EN: The young painter from your class excitedly opens the gate, his messy hair and paint-splattered clothes covered in various colors. He is the undisputed master of this villa.

CN: 之前你给我说过你家的住址，只是没想到富顿街116号居然是个……
EN: You told me your address before, but I never expected 116 Futton Street to be a—

CN: 皮克曼疑惑地低下头，仿佛第一次发现自己那些野兽般的肢体。
EN: Pickman lowered his head in confusion, as if he were discovering his bestial appendages for the first time.

CN: 手和脚？哦，那个……我正在研究一些兽化的人体创作，所以、所以给自己做了些特别的装扮。没错，装扮！
EN: Hands and feet? Oh, that... I'm currently researching some beastly body transformations, so—so I made some special decorations for myself. That's right, decorations!

CN: 这里或许曾是个富丽的宅邸，但那些名贵的器具与装潢，全部都被狂乱的画作与雕塑模型取代。
EN: This place may have been a lavish mansion once, but all the valuable furnishings and decorations have been replaced by frenzied paintings and sculpted figures.

CN: 嗯，这房间真是有你的风格……
EN: Hmm, this room really is your style...

CN: 啊，抱歉抱歉，我平时没有时间收拾，那些佣人也不太愿意来我这里，所以稍稍有些乱……
EN: Ah, sorry, sorry. I usually don't have time to tidy up, and servants aren't too keen on coming here, so it's a bit messy…

CN: 我喜欢这个地方，既安静，又能找到许多的模特。你知道，我想找个人来当模特实在是困难……
EN: I like this place; it's quiet and I can find plenty of models here. Finding someone to be a model is really hard, you know…

CN: 模特？在墓园里找？
EN: A model? You're looking for one in a graveyard?

CN: 啊，不说那个……我邀请了你那么多次，怎么今天突然想起到我家来做客啦！你今天不是要参加画廊的画展吗？
EN: Ah, never mind that... I've invited you so many times—why did you suddenly decide to visit my place? Aren't you supposed to attend the gallery exhibition today?

CN: 也没提前和我说下，我都没时间好好准备……
EN: You didn't even give me a heads-up, so I didn't have time to prepare properly...

CN: 你还好吧，皮克曼？前几天的审核会上，马修先生对你说的那些批评的话，实在是有些过分……
EN: Are you okay, Pickman? What Mr. Matthew said to you at the review meeting a few days ago really was a bit too much...

CN: 我和你接触不算很多，不过我知道，你在绘画上的天赋是有多么的惊人。
EN: I haven't interacted with you often, but I know how astonishing your painting talents are.

CN: 你全身心热爱艺术，又是一个十分敏感的人，我担心你会因为马修先生的评价……
EN: You're sincerely passionate about art and a very sensitive individual. I'm concerned that Mr. Matthew's remarks might've affected you…

CN: 皮克曼瞪大眼睛，浑身颤抖起来。
EN: Pickman's eyes widened, his whole body starting to shake.

CN: 皮克曼？你怎么了……
EN: Pickman? What's wrong…?

CN: 埃弗先生！你是这个世界上最懂我的人！能遇到你是我作为人类以来最幸福的事！
EN: Mr. Avery! You are the one person who understands me the most in this world! Meeting you has been the happiest thing in my life as a human!

CN: 作为人类？……啊！皮克曼先生……我有点……喘不过气……
EN: As a human? ...Ah! Mr. Pickman... I'm a bit... out of breath...

CN: 皮克曼松开了差点被勒死的你，重重拍了拍你的肩膀。你似乎听见了骨头碎裂的声音。
EN: Pickman released his grip on you, nearly strangled in his embrace, and gave your shoulder an intense pat. You seemed to hear the sound of bones cracking.

CN: 我有一个天大的秘密，本来不打算让任何人知道。但是，埃弗先生，你和别人不一样！放心，我一定不会让你白来一趟！请跟我来！
EN: I have a huge secret that I never intended to tell anyone. However, Mr. Avery, you are different from the rest! Rest assured, I promise I won't let your visit be in vain! Please, follow me!

CN: 皮克曼将你请到沙发上坐下，神秘地走到房间中央的画布前。
EN: Pickman invites you to sit on the sofa and enigmatically walks to a canvas at the center of the room.

CN: 是想给我看你最新的画作吗？这我的确很有兴趣。
EN: Are you trying to show me your latest painting? Indeed, I'm interested.

CN: 明天的艺术研讨会，我也准备再次向评审会举荐你的作品，对于革新派来说，你的画作至关重要……
EN: At tomorrow's art symposium, I'm preparing to recommend your work to the jury again. Your paintings are crucial for the Reformists—

CN: 你猜对了一半……我要给你看的，不只是我的画作。我要带你，去看看真正的艺术，真正的世界……
EN: You guessed half right... What I'm going to show you isn't just one of my paintings. I want to take you to see true art, the real world...

CN: 皮克曼克制着自己的兴奋，神秘地扯下黑色的画布。
EN: Pickman held back his elation and pulled off the canvas' black shroud.

CN: 画布之下，你看到一座城市模糊的轮廓。你想要仔细观赏它，却发现自己的眼睛无论如何也无法在它上面聚焦。
EN: Beneath it, you see the blurred outline of a city. You try to examine it in detail, but find that your eyes are unable to focus on it no matter what you do.

CN: 抱歉，我可能今天累得有些眼花了……
EN: Sorry, I might be a bit tired today—my vision's a bit blurry...

CN: 没关系，埃弗。仔细地看，再仔细一些……
EN: It's fine, Avery. Look close, even closer...

CN: 你深吸一口气，紧紧盯着那张模糊的画。
EN: You took a deep breath and stared intently at the obscure painting.

CN: 颜料在旋转。线条在旋转。房间在旋转。模糊的光影从画作上蔓延到整个世界，侵入你的脑海。整个宇宙在旋转。
EN: The paint swirls. The lines swirl. The room swirls. Blurred light and shadows spread from the painting across the entire world, invading your mind. The whole universe swirls and churns.

CN: 在这模糊与旋转里，你重重地栽倒在地……
EN: Caught in the bleary whirl, you fall heavily to the ground...
Ave…

CN: 这……这是哪里？！……
EN: Where... where is this?!...

CN: 「野兽主义」酒馆。这不是你一直想来的地方吗？
EN: Fauvist Pub. Isn't this the place you've always wanted to visit?

CN: 「野兽主义」……酒馆……我刚刚好像在你家沙发上睡着了？我们是什么时候……
EN: Fauvist... Pub... Didn’t I just doze off on your couch? When did we…

CN: 我家的沙发？我的天呐，埃弗，你昨晚到底是喝了多少？你在我家睡觉都已经是前天的事情啦！
EN: My couch? Oh my God, Avery, how much did you drink last night? You stayed over at my place two nights ago!

CN: 昨天你在酒馆里喝醉后的样子，可是征服了这座城邦里的不少人呢！你知道你邀请了多少位女郎给你当模特吗？
EN: The way you acted after getting drunk at the tavern yesterday charmed quite a few people in this city! Do you know how many ladies were invited to model for you?

CN: 我？喝酒？
EN: Me? Drinking?

CN: 这家酒馆的酒都快被你喝光了。可惜我对酒精严重过敏，要是我也能像你一样醉得天昏地暗，我一定能创作出更好的画作！
EN: You've nearly emptied all the booze in this tavern. Unfortunately, I'm severely allergic to alcohol. If I could get as intoxicated as you, I'm sure I could create even more impressive artwork!

CN: 这片奇异的空间和那些你从未有过的记忆让你感到荒谬。可皮克曼言之凿凿的话语，大脑的晕眩，独属于宿醉醒来的口干舌燥……
EN: This bizarre space and the memories you've never experienced before make you feel absurd. However, Pickman's assertive words, the dizziness in your head, and the unique dryness of waking up from a hangover…

CN: 你一边怀疑自己记忆的真实性，一边摸索着把桌上的水杯拿到嘴边，大口喝起来。
EN: You question your memories while fumbling to bring a cup of water on the table to your lips, taking large sips.
!!! Puh—

CN: 这……这些……
EN: This... these...

CN: 你看着从嘴里喷出的液体。那是不知道来自哪个年代的血液，菌群与蠕虫在里面狂欢。
EN: You look at the liquid spurting from your mouth: it's blood from an unknown era, swarming with reveling microbes and wriggling worms inside.

CN: 昨晚我们一起喝酒的时候，您看上去可比现在欢快多了。
EN: You seemed much more cheerful over drinks last night than you do now.

CN: 我的酒深受顾客们的喜爱，但再好的酒，喝得太多，也会变成毒药的……
EN: My wine is well loved by customers, but no matter how good the wine is, drinking too much can turn it into poison...

CN: 啊，抱歉，老板，我现在有些不舒服……
EN: Ah, sorry, madam. I'm not feeling well right now…

CN: 你抬起头。沿着优雅的曲线与精致的身体，你看到一张长着黄色复眼的蝴蝶般的脸。
EN: You lift your head. Atop graceful curves and an exquisite body is a butterfly-like visage with yellow compound eyes.

CN: 啊，抱歉，埃弗先生，昨天累了一夜，我的妆还没有来得及补上。
EN: Ah, pardon me, Mr. Avery. I was worn out from last night and haven't had the chance to fix my makeup yet.

CN: 好了埃弗，别再盯着人家老板看了，多不礼貌。
EN: Alright, Avery. Stop staring at the owner, it's quite impolite.

CN: 今天是城邦的庆典日，王的欢宴晚上就要开始，街上有好多新奇的东西，说不定我们要找的灵感就在哪个转角等着我们呢！
EN: Today is the city's celebration day. The king's feast starts tonight and there are many novelties in the streets; perhaps the epiphany we're searching for is waiting right around the corner!

CN: 走吧走吧，再不去就来不及了！
EN: Let's go, let's go! If we don't hurry, we'll miss it!

CN: 没等你回复，皮克曼不由分说地拉上你离开了酒馆。你无意中回头望了一眼，停下了脚步。
EN: Before you could respond, Pickman pulled you out of the pub without a word. You subconsciously glanced back and came to a halt.

CN: 很棒对吧？你昨天刚来的时候，可兴奋得不得了哪！
EN: Great, right? You were absolutely elated when we arrived yesterday!

CN: <Red:<Italic:一个巨大的胃袋制成的酒馆在你身后轻轻蠕动，酸蚀的液体不时滴落，将那块写着野兽主义的招牌腐蚀得千疮百孔。>>
EN: <Red:<Italic:Fashioned from stomach lining, the massive tavern gently writhes behind you, its biting acid occasionally dripping and corroding a sign that reads "Fauvism" in a thousand perforations.>>

CN: <Yellow:<Italic:蝴蝶女郎依靠在胃袋的入口，向你们热情地挥手。>>
EN: <Yellow:<Italic:The butterfly lady leaning against the stomach's entrance waved enthusiastically at you.>>

CN: 皮克曼在你身前滔滔不绝，你完全没有听见他在说些什么。
EN: Pickman is talking incessantly in front of you as well, but you can't hear a word he's saying.

CN: 你神情恍惚地转过身，跟在他身后，麻木地向这座诡异城邦的深处走去。
EN: You turn around in a daze, following him numbly as you walk deeper into the eerie city.

CN: <Red:<Italic:你最后一次望向天空。原本应该是月亮所在的位置，一只血红色、血管暴凸的眼球正在注视着你。>>
EN: <Red:<Italic:You take one last look at the sky. A blood-red, veiny eye bulges and stares at you where the moon should be.>>

CN: <Red:<Italic:无论你们走向哪里、在做什么，它的目光都牢牢锁定在你的身上。你尝试与它对视，尝试将它遗忘，但所有的努力都无功而返。>>
EN: <Red:<Italic:No matter where you go or what you do, its gaze is fixed firmly on you. You try to meet its gaze—try to forget it—but all efforts are in vain.>>

CN: 血肉盆栽，纯人体制作，生命的奇迹！给它一段故事，它会为你欢笑！给它一滴鲜血，它会为你开出最美的花！
EN: Caro Bonsai, a purely man-made miracle of HP! Give it a story and it will laugh for you! Give it a drop of Bloodfeast and it will bloom the most beautiful flower for you!

CN: 由著名工匠弗兰手工雕刻的颅骨杯盏，最后十个，售完即止。退后退后，一个个来。
EN: Handcrafted by the renowned artisan Fran, this skull-shaped chalice is one of the last ten pieces—once they're gone, they're gone! Step back, step back. One at a time.

CN: 记忆之花的花粉，为您编织一段亦真亦假、如梦似幻的人生。两位，想要体验一下吗？
EN: The Flower of Memory's pollen weaves a life for you that is both tangible and illusory, dreamlike and surreal. You two—would you like to experience it?

CN: 怎么样，埃弗！想要试试吗？
EN: How about it, Avery? Want to give it a try?

CN: 还是算了吧……这个商品听上去和我现在的处境似乎没多大区别，谢谢……
EN: Forget it... What it offers isn't much different from my current situation. Thanks anyways...

CN: 哎？那是什么！
EN: Huh? What is that?

CN: 你随着皮克曼的目光望去。一个花束般的女人身前的流动商摊上，堆着一堆如活物般扭动的颜料。
EN: You follow Pickman's gaze. At a traveling merchant's stall lay a pile of paints, writhing like living creatures, in front of a woman resembling a bouquet.

CN: 五颜六色的颜料，每种颜色都如同这个世界一般黏腻、肮脏、让你感到胃部被某种东西狠狠攥紧。
EN: It's a colorful array of paints; each hue, as sticky and dirty as this grotesque world, makes you feel as though something is gripping your stomach ever-so-tightly.

CN: 两位，要来试试吗？人与世界之间总是隔着一层厚重的涂料。抹上这些由活体生物特制的颜料，让你们与城邦融为一体，靠近这个世界的本质……
EN: Gentlemen, would you like to give it a try? There is always a thick coat of paint separating humans from the truth of this world. Apply these specially-crafted pigments made from the living, merge into the city, and get closer to its essence…

CN: 靠近……世界的本质？！
EN: Closer... to the essence of this world?!

CN: 店铺的老板拿起颜料，倾倒在自己身上。
EN: The shopkeeper picks up the paint and pours it over themself.

CN: 让自己贴近于这个世界的色彩，才能触及到这个世界的真实。您不觉得吗，画家先生？
EN: Only by attuning yourself to the colors of this world can you touch its truth. Don't you think so, Mr. Painter?

CN: 皮克曼，我先说好，我可不想陪你玩这个……
EN: Pickman, let me make one thing clear: I have no intention of playing along with this.

CN: 这怎么行，埃弗！你可是一位革新派的艺术家！
EN: How can this be, Avery?! You're an innovative artist!

CN: 一个能贴近世界本质的机会，一个能创作出绝佳艺术的机会，你怎么忍心放弃它！
EN: A chance to approach the essence of the world, a chance to create exceptional art—you really can't bear to give it up!

CN: 十罐活体颜料已经为您准备好了，一共只需一百二十块指骨。
EN: Ten cans of living paint have been prepared for you. The total cost is only a hundred and twenty finger bones.

CN: ……离开野兽主义酒馆的时候，我把身上那些碎骨全部拿给老板娘了。不过先别纠结颜料的事情了，皮克曼。
EN: ...When we left the Fauvist Pub, I handed all the broken bones I had to the innkeep. Let's not get caught up in the pigments for now, Pickman.

CN: 我们已经从早上逛到了中午，想要进入王邸参加宴会，看上去好像遥遥无期。我们现在一点头绪都还没有。
EN: We've been wandering since morning, hoping to enter the royal mansion for the banquet, but it seems like an endless wait. Right now, we're clueless.

CN: 不用担心，我的挚友！机会就像灵感，作为你最忠诚的朋友，永远会在你最绝望的时候向你伸出援手……
EN: Don't worry, my bosom friend! Opportunities, like inspiration, will always reach out to you at your most desperate moments, as your most loyal ally…

CN: 皮克曼正陶醉在自己的畅谈里，一张肮脏杂乱的海报随风飘扬，覆在他的脸上，堵上了他的嘴。
EN: Pickman was immersed in his own enthusiastic monologue when a dirty, disheveled poster fluttered in the wind, landing on his face and covering his mouth.

CN: 一大群人围在前方，皮克曼不由分说拉着你凑上前。攒动的人头里，你们奋力往前挤去。
EN: A large crowd had gathered ahead and, without hesitation, Pickman pulled you forward. In the throng of people, you struggled to push your way through.

CN: 一个豪华的赛马场看台下方，四名身着沾满颜料的天鹅绒礼服的猪头人围着一滩泥沼。
EN: Beneath a racetrack's luxurious grandstand, four pig-headed people dressed in paint-stained, velvet tuxedos were gathering around a muddy quagmire.

CN: 他们的獠牙镶着金套，每根鬃毛都精心抹着发蜡，鼻环随着激动的喘息叮当作响。
EN: Their fangs are adorned with gold caps, each strand of their manes meticulously styled with hair wax as their nose rings jingled with every excited breath.

CN: 他沾满泥污的身体与双手在干净的地板上留下一地黑色的肮脏印记。栅栏板上黏结着混有马粪的泥浆，形成混沌的棕黑色团块。
EN: His mud-stained hands and body left a trail of dark, filthy imprints on the clean floor. Its fence boards were coated with mud, mixed with horse dung, and forming chaotic brown-black clumps.

CN: 几片被碾碎的蒲公英嵌在泥中，像溃烂皮肤里滋生的霉菌。右下角赫然印着半个带泥的猪蹄印，如同一枚英武的印章。
EN: A few crushed dandelion petals are embedded in the mud as well, like festering mold growing on skin. A half-formed, mud-covered pig's hoofprint boldly stamped a lower right corner, resembling a grandiose seal.

CN: 衣着端庄的猪头人用蹄尖猛戳泥板。
EN: The smartly dressed pig-headed man forcefully poked the mud slab with the tip of his hoof.

CN: 还有这所谓的「笔触」，不过是直肠排泄物的流体动力学轨迹。这样的东西，也配被称为画？！
EN: And, as for this so-called "brushstroke," it's nothing more than a hydrodynamic trail of rectal excrement. How can something like this even be called art?!

CN: 他站起身，居高临下地指着地上的泥点。
EN: He stood up, pointing down at the muddy spot on the ground.

CN: 瞧这泥浆滴落的偶然性，每一道污痕都是虚无与存在的辩论。
EN: Behold the randomness of dripping mud—each stain is a debate between the void and existence.

CN: 诸君！你们听见腐叶在泥浆里哭泣吗？这腥臊的土腥味……这黏稠的死亡质感……
EN: Gentlemen! Can you hear the rotting leaves weeping in the mud? This foul, fishy, earthy smell... this viscous sensation of death...

CN: 看呐！左上方那团蛙卵状气泡……正是被物欲窒息的灵性！
EN: Look! That bubble, shaped like a frog egg in the upper left... it's spirituality being suffocated by material desires!

CN: <Shake:啊……这苦咸的滋味，简直就是宗教圣血的转喻！>
EN: <Shake:Ah... this bitter and salty taste must be a metaphor for the holy blood of religion!>

CN: 猪头人们争论不停，聒噪的嚎叫声像是一块修饰的铁片，不停地刮过你们的耳膜。
EN: The pig-headed men argue without end, their noisy squeals like a piece of polished metal constantly scraping against your eardrums.

CN: 离开？为什么要离开？你难道没有感觉到自己的灵感正在迸发吗，埃弗！
EN: Leave? Why would you leave? Can't you feel your inspiration surging, Avery?

CN: 灵感？我不觉得这些猪头人在地上滚出来的污痕有任何灵感可言……
EN: Inspiration? I don't see any insight in the stains left behind by these pig-headed creatures rolling around in the dirt…

CN: 啊哈，埃弗，埃弗，你错了！我们所看到的，正是艺术的一部分呐！
EN: Ah-ha, Avery—Avery, you're mistaken! What we're seeing is part of the art itself!

CN: 你在说什么，埃弗？我们在参与的，不正是一场艺术沙龙嘛！这场创作游戏，和过往那些艺术沙龙没有任何区别，我的朋友。
EN: What are you talking about, Avery? Aren't we witnessing an art salon right now? This creative venture is no different from the art salons of the past, my friend.

CN: 你震惊地望向皮克曼。他没有理会你，推开人群走上前去，学起了那些猪头人，捧起地上的污泥。
EN: You looked at Pickman in shock. He ignored you, pushed through the crowd, and imitated those pig-headed creatures, scooping up mud from the ground.

CN: 艺术的光芒无处不在。能抓住它的人并不多。猪头先生们有他们自己的理解方式，这没什么。每个人眼里的世界，理应是不一样的……
EN: The brilliance of art is everywhere; few can truly grasp it. The pig-headed gentlemen have their own way of understanding and that's perfectly fine. The world should look different through each person's eye, after all...

CN: 皮克曼一边说着，一边将那些污泥用他特有的方式涂抹在广场的每一个角落。
EN: Pickman said this while applying sludge to every corner of the plaza in his unique way.

CN: 夸张的构图，凌乱的线条，疯狂的色彩……一幅巨大的食尸鬼宴饮图很快在广场上呈现出来。
EN: Exaggerated composition, chaotic lines, mad colors... A grand painting of a ghoul feast quickly emerges in the plaza.

CN: 那些活灵活现的食尸鬼们倚靠着广场的石柱，坐在弧形的石阶上，以泥污中心的画家们与围观的人群为餐食。
EN: Lifelike ghouls lean against the stone pillars of the plaza, sitting on the curved stone steps, feasting on painters at the center of the filth and its surrounding crowd.

CN: 不需要去批判任何东西，埃弗。一个艺术家需要做的，是在一切事物中寻找艺术的灵感！
EN: There's no need to criticize everything, Avery. What an artist must do is find inspiration in all things!

CN: 我是城邦王邸的管家。现在，我将代表城邦的王，邀请您与您的朋友作为尊贵的特邀画师，参加王邸的晚宴。
EN: I am the butler of the city's royal residence. Now, on behalf of the king, I invite you and your friend to the royal residence as esteemed guest artists to attend the banquet.

CN: 您赢下了这场比赛，当之无愧，皮克曼先生。
EN: You have won this position, Mr. Pickman, and you truly deserve it.

CN: 真、真的吗？！你听见了吗，埃弗！
EN: Really? Really?! Did you hear that, Avery?

CN: 不！这怎么可能！他的画作没有任何先锋的理念传递！
EN: No! How is this possible?! His painting doesn't convey any avant-garde concepts!

CN: 空间与形体构造简直一团糟！王怎么可能喜欢这样的垃圾？
EN: The use of space and form is a complete mess! How could the king possibly enjoy such garbage?

CN: 一位画家向另一位画家冲去，一只猪头人与另一只猪头人扭打在一起。
EN: One painter charged at another painter while a pig-headed creature wrestled with another of its kind.

CN: 很快，泥潭中斗殴声四起，泥点飞溅，主体与结构统统涣散、消失，为这幅画卷添上了一笔超越时代的后现代笔触。
EN: Soon, sounds of brawling erupted from the muck. Sludge spattered everywhere as the main figures and structures dissolved and vanished, adding a postmodern touch to the painting that transcended its time.

CN: 王邸的管家没有理会他们，侧身让到一边，向路的前方恭敬地伸出手臂。
EN: The butler of the royal residence ignored them, stepped aside, and respectfully extended his arm to the path ahead.

CN: 请吧，尊贵的客人。城邦的王，正在王邸中恭候二位的到来。
EN: If you please, esteemed guests, the king of the city is awaiting your arrival at the Ossuary Palace.

CN: 啊！@3！城邦伟大的王！您的身躯如王邸般伟岸！您的声音如蜂蜜般甘甜！您的……
EN: Ah! @3! O great king of Necrovia! Your body is as majestic as a royal palace! Your voice is as sweet as honey! Your—

CN: 好了，好了，不要再卖弄你那厚得像城墙一样的脸皮了。我们的陛下是不会因为你这些肤浅的夸赞而产生任何动摇的。
EN: Enough, enough—stop flaunting your masque as dense as a city wall. Our king will not experience any thrill from your superficial flattery.

CN: 陛下拥有太阳般明亮的双眼和满月般洁净的心，他知道，谁才是身边最值得信赖之人……
EN: His Majesty possesses eyes as bright as the sun and a heart as pure as the full moon. He knows who the most trustworthy person by his side truly is...

CN: 粗鲁的贵族被舞女牵起来向前走去。她回过头勾勾手指，矜持的贵族放下酒杯，整理了一下自己优雅的衣领，手背在身后走向舞池。
EN: The rude noble was ushered forward. The dancer turned her head and beckoned with her finger; the reserved noble set down his glass, adjusted his elegant collar, and walked toward the dance floor with his hands behind his back.

CN: 城邦的王轻轻拍了拍舞女的脸颊，向她摆了摆手。舞女脸上的失落转瞬即逝。很快，扭动的腰肢重新回到她忠诚的舞池。
EN: The king gently patted the dancer's cheek and waved goodbye to her. The disappointment on her face vanished in an instant. Soon, her swaying figure returned to its dedicated dance floor.

CN: 舞池的氛围随着音乐而愈发浓烈。贵族，士兵，画家，舞者……无数长着野兽头颅的高级动物塞满舞池。
EN: The atmosphere of the dance floor became increasingly more intense as the music played. Nobles, soldiers, painters, dancers... countless sapient creatures with beast heads fill the dance floor.

CN: 我的天呐……这种味道，他们、他们是在干什么？……
EN: My goodness... This smell... What are they doing?

CN: 宫廷舞会嘛，还能干什么？
EN: A royal dance, what else would they do?

CN: 哎哎，埃弗，你看！狂欢的舞池和无聊的君主，多么有趣的构图！下一幅画我就准备用这个作为主题……
EN: Hey, Avery, look! The lively dance floor juxtaposed with the bored monarch—what an intriguing composition! I'm going to use this as the theme for my next painting...

CN: 啊！画家皮克曼，画家埃弗！我代表这座城邦，欢迎你们的到来。
EN: Ah! Mr. Pickman, Mr. Avery! On behalf of this city, I welcome your arrival.

CN: 在你们交谈的时候，管家凑到城邦君主面前低语了几句。无聊的君主眼睛立刻亮起来，从人群中捕捉到你们所在的位置。
EN: While you were talking, the housekeeper leaned closer to his king and whispered a few words. The bored monarch's eyes lit up immediately, scanning the crowd to pinpoint your whereabouts.

CN: 主宾位？！这两个人是什么身份，凭什么能坐到主宾位！
EN: Main guest seats?! What status do these two individuals have to deserve main guest seats?!

CN: 哼，两个能说会道的弄臣……哎，我们可怜的王，是被奸人所蒙蔽了啊……
EN: Hmph, two eloquent jesters... Alas, our poor king has been deceived by miscreants…

CN: 呀，两位艺术家，宴饮结束后的舞会，我会等着你们哦……
EN: Ah, you two artists, I'll be waiting on the dancefloor after the feast…

CN: 穿过由嫉妒与讨好的窃窃私语组成的蛛网、挤开由黏腻的肢体与野蛮的面庞汇成的人潮，你们来到王身边的座位坐下。
EN: Navigating through the web of whispers, filled with envy and flattery, you make your way past a throng of sticky bodies and savage faces to find your seats next to the king.

CN: 还好还好，君主先生。这样的聚会我见过太多啦。沉闷，俗套，无聊，与艺术的真理背道而驰……
EN: Not bad, not bad, Mr. Monarch. I've seen too many gatherings like this. Dull, cliché, boring... They go against the true essence of art…

CN: 喂！皮克曼，你……
EN: Hey! Pickman, you—

CN: 哈哈哈哈……皮克曼先生，端起你的酒杯，喝下这杯开胃酒吧。这是你应得的恩赏。
EN: Hahaha... Mr. Pickman, raise your glass and drink this aperitif. It is the reward you deserve.

CN: 君主嘲讽的眼睛扫过舞池里那一张张谄媚的脸。
EN: The monarch's taunting eyes swept over the sycophantic figures in the dancehall.

CN: 舞池里的这些人，你们对谁感兴趣或是厌恶？那个精明的侯爵？还是那个聪明的舞女？
EN: Who among those on the dancefloor catches your interest or draws your disdain? The shrewd marquis or the clever performer?

CN: 呵呵呵呵，哈哈哈哈。你的笨拙和皮克曼的机灵一样动人，埃弗先生。
EN: Hehehe, hahaha! Your cluelessness is as charming as Pickman's clarity, Mr. Avery.

CN: 我们总是需要按照某些规则去做事，城邦的王也不例外。所以请放心，这些家伙是不会被送上我们的餐桌的。
EN: We always need to follow a certain etiquette when handling our affairs; kings of cities are no exception. I assure you, these fellows will not be served on our dining table.

CN: 今天的食材，来自一种与我们类似的生物。
EN: Today's ingredients come from a beast similar to us.

CN: 这种被用做食材的生物同我们一样，有着灿烂的历史、辉煌的文明、卓越的智慧以及细腻的肉质。
EN: This ingredient, like us, has a splendid history, magnificent civilization, exceptional wisdom, and delicate meat.

CN: 遗憾的是，他们的饮食习惯与我们截然不同。人类总是会因为不同的理由而战斗，在这场因饮食而展开的战争里，我的军队踏破了他们的王庭。
EN: Unfortunately, their dietary habits are completely different from our own. Humans always find reasons to battle and, in this war sparked by dietary differences, my army has stormed their royal court.

CN: 胜利者有权享用败者的一切。弱肉强食，这是这个世界运转规则里难得有趣的那一部分。
EN: Our battle's victor has the right to bask in everything the defeated possesses. Survival of the fittest—it's one of the more interesting aspects of how this world operates.

CN: 君王微笑着拍了拍手。如同一颗石子轻轻投入湖面，温柔的涟漪带着王的谕令散开。
EN: The monarch smiled and gently clapped his hands. Like a pebble lightly cast into a lake, gentle ripples carried the king's decree outward.

CN: 长着兽头的贵族们不再高谈阔论，蛇身舞女与兽人卫兵停止了交媾。波纹所至之处，属于人间的一切声音都停了下来。
EN: The beast-headed nobles no longer spoke with grandeur, the snake-bodied dancers and beastman guards ceased their dalliance. Wherever the ripples reached, all sounds belonging to the human realm came to a halt.

CN: 一队衣冠楚楚的鬣狗侍者从后厨鱼贯而出，将一百口大锅放在王巨大的餐桌之上。
EN: A line of well-dressed hyena servants streams out of the kitchen, placing one hundred large cauldrons on the king's enormous dining table.

CN: 权力，欢爱，战斗，创作……这些美妙的词汇已经让我感到厌倦，幸运的是，这世上依然有值得期待的东西。现在，它们已被送上餐桌。
EN: Power, love, conflict, creation... these wonderful words have worn me out. Fortunately, there are still things worth looking forward to in this world. Now, they have been served on this table.

CN: 为它们欢呼，为它们祈祷。<Big:拿出你们全部的激情与欲望，享用它们吧。>
EN: Cheer for them, pray for them. <Big:Pour out all your passion—your desire—and enjoy them.>

CN: 王的话语落下，人群爆发出一阵强烈的欢呼。上百口锅被同时揭开，一股混合着浓烈香料与腐肉的浓郁气息在整个花园中弥漫开。
EN: The king's words fell over the crowd, soon bursting into a wave of fervent cheers. Over a hundred pots were simultaneously uncovered, releasing a rich aroma that blended intense spices with rotting meat and spread throughout the garden.
This is—?!

CN: 暗红色的利爪绝望地伸出锅面，布满咬痕的肋骨在粘稠的汤汁中翻涌。
EN: Dark red claws desperately extend out of the pot before you. Bite-marked ribs churn in thick broth.

CN: 呵，真是毫无体面……嘿！那是王给我的恩赏！给我滚开！！
EN: Hah, what a total disgrace... Hey! That's a reward bestowed upon me by the king! Back off!!

CN: 枪头逼开对手，刺入目标的胸膛。一块块煮熟的躯体从锅中被捞起，囫囵塞进他们留着涎水的嘴里。
EN: Spearheads push aside rivals and pierce their targets' chests. Pieces of cooked cadaver are scooped from the pots and stuffed whole into slavering mouths.

CN: 皮克曼一边大快朵颐，一边将餐盘递到你面前。一只食尸鬼的利爪摊开来躺在餐盘中央，像是来自舞伴的邀请。
EN: Pickman, feasting heartily, extends a plate towards you. A ghoul's claw lies spread open in the center of the plate, as if it were an invitation from a dance partner.

CN: 你深吸一口气，拿起那只暗红的利爪，犹疑着放入嘴中。
EN: You took a deep breath, picked up the dark red claw, and hesitated before putting it in your mouth.

CN: 怎么样，埃弗？没有骗你吧！
EN: How is it, Avery? I didn't deceive you, did I?

CN: 你还真别说，皮克曼……这些食物虽然猎奇，但是味道的确还不错……
EN: You know what, Pickman... These dishes may be quirky, but they actually taste pretty good...

CN: 你不再像开始时那样艰难地品尝。你咀嚼的速度越来越快，再也顾不上体面，扔掉了手里的刀叉，用双手撕扯起来。
EN: You no longer savor the food as strenuously as you did before. Your chewing speed increases, as you can no longer afford to be polite. You drop the knife and fork in your grip and start tearing the food apart with both hands.

CN: 嘿！你干什么！这是王为我们准备的食物！走开！！
EN: Hey! What are you doing?! This is the food the king prepared for us! Get away!

CN: 你能看到这个世界疯狂的美……你有着出众的天赋，埃弗！
EN: You can discern madness in the beauty of this world... You have outstanding talent, Avery!

CN: 这些，不是食尸鬼的肉吗……
EN: Isn't this the flesh of ghouls...?

CN: 你惊愕地抬起头。一瞬间，整个王宫花园安静下来。所有的人都停止了进食，向你望了过来。
EN: You look up in shock. For a moment, the entire palace garden falls silent. Everyone stops eating to look directly at you.

CN: 城邦的王也放下了手中的餐叉，咧着嘴望向你们，如同在看着一幕即将进入高潮的好戏。
EN: The king puts down the fork in his hand, grinning as his gaze turns to you. It was as though he were watching an amusing performance about to reach its climax.

CN: 你是什么意思……什么叫……你眼中的世界？
EN: What do you mean...? What does the world look like in your eyes?

CN: 你错愕地停下，抬起头痴痴地望向他。
EN: You're petrified in shock, looking up at him with a dazed expression.

CN: 皮克曼兴奋起来，踩上餐桌，张开他的双臂——
EN: Pickman is elated, stepping onto the dining table, spreading his arms wide—

CN: 欢迎，埃弗！欢迎来到不受待见的画家，皮克曼眼中的世界！！
EN: Welcome, Avery! Welcome to the world seen through the eyes of the unappreciated painter, Pickman!

CN: 随着皮克曼纵情的高呼，你感到手里的食物手感开始变化。你低下头——
EN: With Pickman's exuberant shout, you felt the texture of the food in your hand start to change. You looked down—

CN: 一个光滑的人头被你抱在手上，上面还残留着你啃食的齿印。你惊叫着将它远远扔开，再抬起头时，你感到一阵强烈的晕眩。
EN: A smooth human head is held in your hands, still bearing the bite marks from your gnawing. You scream and throw it far away; when you lift your head again, you feel an overwhelming dizziness.

CN: 暗淡的色彩，明亮的色彩，荒诞的色彩，真实的色彩……无数色彩汇集在你眼前。
EN: Dull colors, bright colors, absurd colors, true colors... An array of shades converge before your eyes.

CN: 现实的世界与梦中的世界一同从色彩扭曲的缝隙中钻出，不同世界的光影涌入你狭窄的大脑中，剧烈的疼痛几乎要将你撕裂。
EN: The real and illusory worlds merge together from their distorted, colorful seams; the light and shadow of different realms rush into your compact brain, the intense pain almost rending you to pieces.

CN: 痛苦吗，埃弗？不要担心……这是天才将要承受的代价……
EN: Is it painful, Avery? Don't worry... It's the price a genius must bear...

CN: 什么是真实？什么是虚假？什么是食尸鬼？什么是人？
EN: What is true? What is false? What is a ghoul? What is a human?

CN: 皮克曼的话如同一句咒语。在那混乱的色彩中，一切都扭曲着改变了。
EN: Pickman's words are like a spell. In that chaos of colors, everything around you starts distorting and changing.

CN: 蛆虫变为了美丽的舞娘，兽头人变成了宫廷的王公大臣，疯狂的、现实的、梦境般的画面在你眼前互相交织。
EN: Maggots transformed into beautiful dancers, beast-headed men became the court's royal ministers, and scenes of madness, reality, and dreams coalesced before your eyes.

CN: 那位君主在王座上纵情欢笑，拍着手站起身来。
EN: The ruler on the throne indulged in unfettered mirth, clapping his hands and standing before you.

CN: 你再也支撑不住，将手里的人头扔在脚下，在宫廷贵族们惊愕的眼神中夺路而逃。
EN: You can no longer hold on, kicking the head at your feet and fleeing along the path under the shocked gazes of the court nobles.

CN: 咦？这位先生，请问……啊！……
EN: Huh? Sir, may I ask... Ah!

CN: 急促的脚步声没有片刻停息。你一把撞开拦住你的两个人类卫兵，冲出了这座堂皇的宫殿。
EN: The hurried footsteps didn't stop, even for a moment. You shoved aside the two human guards blocking your way and burst out of the grand palace.

CN: 沿着那条和来时完全不一样的路，你向这座城邦的出口跑去。
EN: You ran to find a way out of the city, following a completely different path than the one you'd tread before.

CN: 跟随着你的脚步，周遭的一切都变化了。画作般的世界变成了你熟悉的城市，天上巨大的眼睛变成了明亮的月亮。
EN: Following your footsteps, everything around you changes. The world around you—once a painting—transforms into the city you are familiar with, the giant eye in the sky becoming a bright moon once more.

CN: 你惊恐地看着颜料如同被雨水冲刷般退却，留下世界真实的样貌。而如今，这些真实只让你感到恐慌。
EN: You watch in horror as the paint recedes like rain being washed away, revealing the truth of the world. Now, however, this truth only fills you with panic.

CN: 可在基础的视觉构造上，它的处理实在是不负责任且荒谬的。
EN: However, its approach to visual fundamentals is truly irresponsible and absurd.

CN: 不过我得勉强承认，比起皮克曼那些疯狂的表达，你的画起码还是具备一定的鉴赏价值……
EN: Well, I have to reluctantly admit that compared to Pickman's expressionistic madness, your paintings at least have some admirable qualities…

CN: 哦，先生，我们还是不要谈论那个家伙了。他的画作，简直就是对人类的背叛。
EN: Oh, sir, let's not talk about that guy anymore. His artwork is nothing short of a betrayal to humanity.

CN: 我赞同！象征意象的使用是有明确的逻辑与边界的。皮克曼那些关于怪物的污秽表达，所表达的是一种绝不能被人类文明接受的艺术观。
EN: I agree! The use of symbolic imagery has clear logic and boundaries. Pickman's filthy expressions of monsters convey an artistic view that cannot be accepted by human civilization.

CN: 那绝不是一名人类对食尸鬼的刻画，那是……一头真正的食尸鬼对人类世界的窥探！
EN: That's definitely not a human's depiction of a ghoul—it's that of a true ghoul's peeping into the human world!

CN: 哦，嘿！那不是埃弗先生吗？今年美术学院最优秀的新生！
EN: Oh, hey! Isn't that Mr. Avery? This year's most promising Art Academy freshman!

CN: 埃弗先生，画展刚结束就不见你人了。要加入我们的艺术沙龙吗？
EN: Mr. Avery, you were nowhere to be found after the art exhibition ended. Would you like to join our art salon?

CN: 臭气熏天的露天广场消失了。四名衣冠楚楚的画家坐在一间精致的画廊里高谈阔论，其中一名画家体面地起身向你招招手。
EN: The stench-filled plaza disappeared. Four well-dressed painters sat in an exquisite gallery, engaging in lofty conversation, and one of the painters rose gracefully to wave at you.

CN: 埃弗先生怎么回事，怎么跑得满头大汗……这是一种什么新潮的艺术形式吗？
EN: Mr. Avery, what's going on? Why are you running around drenched in sweat...? Is this some kind of new art trend?

CN: 你没有理会他们，沿着来时的道路，径直向前跑去。
EN: You ignored them, running straight ahead on the path you came from.

CN: 呀，这不是埃弗先生嘛！听说您和皮克曼先生去了王的宴会……看来，王宫的酒可没有我家的好喝？
EN: Oh, Mr. Avery! I heard you and Mr. Pickman went to the king's banquet... The palace's wine isn't as good as the one at my place, is it?

CN: 胃袋制成的酒馆消失了。那家闪耀着温暖灯火的酒馆门前，美丽的老板娘向你热情地招了招手。
EN: The tavern made of a stomach sac vanished. In front of the pub, glowing with warm lights, the beautiful proprietress waved warmly at you.

CN: 她向你递来一杯酒，澄澈香醇的酒液在干净的玻璃杯中摇晃，在月光的照映下闪闪发光。
EN: She hands you a glass of wine. The clear and fragrant liquid swirling in the clean glass shimmered under the moonlight.

CN: 啊，埃弗先生，看你跑的多么辛苦……进来喝一杯吧？昨晚和你一起玩乐的酒伴们，还在等着你的光临呢！
EN: Ah, Mr. Avery, look how hard you've been running... Come in for a drink? Your drinking companions from last night have been waiting for you!

CN: 你想起那些长着兽头的人们、想起那些泛着腐味的酒液，胃里一阵翻涌。没有余力再理会酒馆的老板，你继续向前跑去。
EN: You recall those people with beastly heads and the wine that reeked of decay, causing your stomach to churn. With no strength left to spare for the tavern keeper, you continue running forward.

CN: 你忘了自己跑了有多久。城邦被你远远甩在身后。身边的高楼变成了坟墓，你缓缓停下脚步，在这片墓园中穿行。
EN: You forgot how long you'd been running. The city is far behind you. The tall buildings that surrounded you have transformed into gravestones. You come to a slow trot, walking through the graveyard.

CN: 很快，那个造型怪异的别墅再次出现在你身前。
EN: Soon, that oddly shaped villa appears before you again.

CN: 在那个涂抹着各种色彩的门牌前，皮克曼忧郁的身影坐在一块坟墓上，望着天上那轮硕大、美丽的月亮。
EN: In front of that sign painted with various colors, Pickman's melancholic figure sits upon a tomb, gazing at the big, beautiful moon in the sky.

CN: 啊，你到现在还觉得是去到了我画里的世界吗？
EN: Ah, do you still believe you've entered the world inside my painting?

CN: 没有画里的世界，埃弗。那幅画，只是让你得到了和我一样的眼睛。一双能看到真实世界的眼睛。
EN: There is no realm within the painting, Avery. That painting merely granted you the same eyes as mine—a pair of eyes capable of seeing the truth of this world.

CN: 什么是真实？什么是虚假？我不知道，埃弗。没有人能够告诉我。
EN: What is true? What is false? I don't know, Avery. No one can tell me.

CN: 现在，你也看到了这个世界……你能告诉我答案吗，我的朋友？
EN: Now that you've seen this part of my world for yourself... Can you tell me the answer, my friend?

CN: 皮克曼蹲坐在墓碑上，双手托着下巴，声音前所未有的平静。
EN: Pickman sat crouched on the tombstone, resting his chin in his hands. His voice was remarkably calm.

CN: 你从坟墓上抬起头，撞上他满是疑惑与好奇的眼睛。
EN: You lift your gaze from the grave and meet his eyes, filled with confusion and curiosity.

CN: 我们所生活的世界才是真实，毋庸置疑的真实，皮克曼……
EN: The world we live in is real beyond any doubt, Pickman...

CN: 哎……我要是能够有你这样坚定，或许就不会给身边的人还有我自己带去那么多的灾难了。
EN: Ah... If I could be as resolute as you, perhaps I wouldn't have brought so much disaster to those around me, including myself.

CN: 皮克曼轻轻叹了口气，利爪般的双腿在墓碑上舒展开，尖利的牙齿在月光下闪耀着。
EN: Pickman let out a soft sigh; his claw-like legs stretched out on the gravestone, his sharp teeth gleaming under the moonlight.

CN: 皮克曼……你……这些不是你的装扮？……
EN: Pickman... you... Is this not a costume?

CN: 不用担心，埃弗。你是我最好的朋友，我不会伤害你。我也不会去伤害任何人……
EN: Don't worry, Avery. You're my best friend; I won't hurt you. I won't harm anyone else, either…

CN: 另一个世界，一个真正疯狂的、美丽的世界，我已经去过了。那是一个属于食尸鬼之王的城邦。那里比我们生活的地方更加疯狂，也更加真实。
EN: Another world, a truly mad and beautiful world—I have already been there. It is a city belonging to the King of Ghouls. It is far madder and far more real than where we live now.

CN: 所以，抱歉，皮克曼。
EN: So... I’m sorry, Pickman.

CN: 这个世界还有很多我怀念的人与事……如果有机会，我还能再来拜访你吗，埃弗？
EN: There are still many people and things in this world that I'll miss... If I get the chance, can I visit you again, Avery?

CN: 我很期待那一天。我很期待，那时候你将为我带来什么样的艺术。那些我一生也无法触碰到的艺术……
EN: I'm looking forward to that day. I'm eager to see what kind of pieces you'll bring me then—art that I could never reach in my lifetime...

CN: 你太看轻自己了，我的朋友。你的潜力，或许连你自己也没有察觉到……你和我，我们本就是一样的人。
EN: You underestimate yourself, my friend. You haven't even realized your own potential... You and I, we are the same kind of person.

CN: 时钟指向午夜。
EN: The clock hand points to midnight.

CN: 你忘记了自己是如何穿过那片墓园、穿过城市的街道，回到自己家中。
EN: You've forgotten how you walked through that cemetery, crossed the city streets, and returned to your own home.

CN: 你尝试着忘记今天所经历的一切。你知道，那是你能够安睡的前提。
EN: You try to forget everything you experienced today. You know that's required for you to sleep soundly.

CN: 真实，虚假……不要再想这些了。祝福皮克曼吧，埃弗。你跟他是不一样的人。你需要躺在现实世界这张大床上，享受一场现实世界的安眠。
EN: Truth, falsehood... stop thinking about these things. Send Pickman your blessings, Avery. You're not the same as him. You need to lie on the big bed of the real world and enjoy a peaceful sleep in the real world.

CN: 你拉开窗帘。明亮清澈的月光照耀在你脸上。你安心地闭上眼。
EN: You pull the curtain open; the bright and clear moonlight shines on your face. You close your eyes, feeling at ease.

CN: 呼，美妙的月光。今晚一定能睡个好觉……
EN: Hmph, the moonlight is beautiful. I'll definitely have a good night's sleep tonight...

CN: 你猛然从床上坐起身。当你重新睁开眼，整个世界变成了如血般粘稠的暗红色。
EN: You startle in bed. When you open your eyes again, the entire world has turned a dark red, thick as blood.

CN: 你望向天空。原本应该是月亮所在的位置，<Big:<Red:一只血红色、血管暴凸的眼球正在注视着你。>>
EN: You gaze at the sky. Where the moon should be, <Big:<Red:a blood-red, veiny eyeball is watching you.>>

CN: <Red:我们本就是一样的人。不是吗，埃弗？>
EN: <Red:We really are the same kind of person. Aren't we, Avery?>

CN: 抬起你宿醉的脑袋，端起你肮脏的酒杯。忘掉昨夜的一切，新的派对就要到来。\n我的朋友，欢迎来到这座表现主义的乐园。
EN: Lift your hungover head and raise your grimy glass. Forget everything from last night; a new party is about to begin.\nWelcome to expressionist paradise, my friend.

CN: 批判不是艺术家的职责，创造才是。
EN: Critique is not the artist's duty—creation is.

CN: 闲话·关于墨菲
EN: Chat: About "Murphy

CN: 你说这些糖果的夹心是怎么装进去的，完全找不到缝隙或者针孔呀，真想让发明这些糖果的人也去弥萨格的餐厅上班，每天都给本小姐发明一种新的甜点！
EN: How do they get the filling inside? I can't find any seams or pinholes! I wish these candy inventors would join Mythag’s staff and invent a new dessert for me every single day!

CN: 软的、硬的、脆的、酥的、奶味的、果味的、坚果味的、巧克力味的……啊，世界上怎么会有这么多种我没吃过的糖果？决定了，向弥萨格提议每周过一次诸圣节吧！
EN: Soft, hard, crunchy, flaky, milky, fruity, nutty, chocolatey... Ah, how can there be so many varieties I haven’t tasted yet? I’ve decided: Mythag must make Halloween a weekly holiday!

CN: 哈哈哈，我的，我的，弥萨格所有糖果都是我的了！还等什么，跟着本女巫走，马上准备去征服整个伦蒂尼恩的糖果店吧！
EN: Hahaha! Mine, all mine! All of Mythag's candies belong to me! What are you waiting for? Follow me, the great Candy Witch! We’re going to conquer every candy shop in Londinium right now!
